task: Improve English proficiency to achieve higher standardized test scores
ref_subtasks: Estimate IELTS Writing Task 2 band and sub-scores (TR/CC/LR/GRA) for my essay pasted below (about whether high earners should donate 10% to charity, inspired by https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PHgdXWZLs9g). Then give 3 practical fixes to raise it by 0.5 band. Essay: [PASTE TEXT].

Band-score (TR/CC/LR/GRA) my IELTS Academic Task 2 essay about charitable donations (inspired by https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PHgdXWZLs9g), correct grammar throughout, and rewrite my introduction plus one body paragraph to Band 7 level. Here is my essay text: [PASTE ESSAY HERE].

Simulate an IELTS Speaking Part 2 test: provide a cue card topic (include bullet prompts and 1-minute prep + 2-minute speaking time). I will record a spoken answer and paste a transcript here. Then score it (Band 0–9) with detailed feedback on Fluency & Coherence, Lexical Resource, Grammar Range & Accuracy, and Pronunciation, plus improvement tips.
I’m studying AP Biology—can you explain the light-dependent reactions step-by-step, then generate 20 Anki-style cloze flashcards and quiz me on the toughest 5?

Create a 2-week IB Theory of Knowledge essay plan for the prompt “How does technology shape what we claim to know?” Include a clear thesis, 3 Areas of Knowledge, 2–3 Knowledge Questions, real-life situation ideas using https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-jcIdZFxLFc and http://www.iconnecthere.com/, and a section-by-section essay outline.
Create a 30-day daily English study schedule for a B1 learner with 20 minutes/day, including listening, speaking prompts, vocab, and weekly self-check tests.
Create a ~700-word TOEFL-style reading passage (total output 1000 words) with 10 very hard questions, bold only quoted evidence in passage, include answers+explanations, balanced A-D.
Role-play a 10-minute restaurant order with me; ask follow-up questions, correct my pronunciation/grammar after each turn, and summarize my top 5 mistakes at the end.

Assess my IELTS Writing Task 1 response describing a chart (not provided): estimate band scores for Task Achievement, Coherence & Cohesion, Lexical Resource, and Grammar; give detailed improvement feedback and a corrected high-band rewrite. Assume I’m a student aiming for Band 7+.

I have 14 days until the IELTS Academic test and need a Band 7 overall; my Reading target is Band 7. Based on my recent practice results (e.g., 26/40 correct in 60 minutes), estimate my current IELTS Reading band and create a day-by-day 2‑week study plan with daily mini mock tests and review tasks.
rubric: The task targets specific standardized test formats and scoring criteria (e.g., TOEFL/IELTS/SAT/ACT), including question types, timing, and test-taking strategies.
The task improves core language skills relevant to test performance: reading comprehension, listening comprehension, speaking fluency/pronunciation, and writing clarity/coherence.
The task builds academic vocabulary and grammar accuracy, emphasizing high-frequency test patterns (e.g., complex sentences, transitions, common error types) and measurable mastery.
The task includes structured, feedback-driven practice (e.g., graded essays/speaking responses, error analysis, targeted drills) with clear performance metrics and iteration.
The task provides a personalized study plan and progress tracking (diagnostic assessment, goal score setting, schedule, and periodic full-length practice tests) to close identified gaps.
